enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative
Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
enriched_requirements.object: immediate effect

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Urban Co-operative Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'banks' or 'UCBs' and individually as a 'bank' or 'UCB').
enriched_requirements.object: Co-operative Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' or 'UCBs' and individually as a 'bank' or 'UCB')

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In this context, 'Urban Co-operative Banks' shall mean Primary Co-operative Banks
as defined under section 5(ccv) read with section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act,
1949.
enriched_requirements.object: as defined under section 5(ccv) read with section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The maintenance of Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) shall be reported to the RBI
under the following statutory returns:
(1) Form B Return for Scheduled Co-operative Banks.
enriched_requirements.object: RBI under the following statutory returns: (1) Form B Return for Scheduled Co-operative Banks

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The maintenance of Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR) shall be reported to RBI under
the statutory return Form I Return (for SLR) for all Co-operative Banks under
Section 24 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, read with Section 56 thereof.
enriched_requirements.object: under the statutory return Form I Return (for SLR) for all Co-operative Banks under Section 24 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949,

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall
bear the meaning assigned to them below:
<image>
(1) 'Aggregate Deposits' shall mean aggregation of demand and time deposits.
enriched_requirements.object: to them below: <image> (1) 'Aggregate Deposits' shall mean aggregation of demand and time deposits

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) 'Apportionment of Saving Bank Account into demand liability and time liability': a
bank shall undertake the apportionment of Saving Bank Account into demand liability
and time liability as per the following procedure:
enriched_requirements.object: Saving Bank Account into demand liability and time liability as per the following procedure:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) A bank is required to calculate the proportion of its savings bank deposits as
at the close of business on March 31 and September 30, into demand and
time liabilities in terms of Regulation 7 of The Reserve Bank of India
enriched_requirements.object: of its savings bank deposits as at the close of business on March 31 and September 30, into demand and time liabilities in

Note: Co-operative Land Mortgage / Development Banks are not part of 'banking
system'

UCBs, shall not include the following:

maintained with the State Co-operative Bank of the State concerned or with the
district central cooperative bank of the district concerned, such balance to the
extent to which it has been drawn against or availed of shall not be deemed to
be cash maintained in India.
enriched_requirements.object: cash maintained in India

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(9) 'Cash in India / Cash in hand' shall consist of total amount of rupee notes and
coins held by bank branches / ATMs / Cash deposit machines maintained by banks
in India, including transit cash on the bank's books as also cash with Business
Correspondents (BCs), but shall exclude cash, where physical possession is with
outsourced vendors / BCs, which is not replenished in the bank's ATM and / or is not
reflected on the bank's books.
enriched_requirements.object: of rupee notes and coins held by bank branches / ATMs / Cash deposit machines maintained by banks in India, including transit cash on the bank's books as also cash with Business Correspondents (BCs), but shall exclude cash

any item other than bank / currency notes, rupee coin (including one rupee notes)
and subsidiary coins current on the date of the posting of the register

maintained in India, namely;

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: Money at Call and Short Notice from outside the banking system shall
be shown against liability to others.
enriched_requirements.object: to others

calendar month or sixteenth day to the last day of each calendar month, both days

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) The average of the minimum balances maintained (in each account) in each
of the months during the half year period shall be treated by the bank as the
amount representing the "time liability" portion of the savings bank deposits.
enriched_requirements.object: bank as the amount representing the "time liability" portion of the savings bank deposits

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) The proportions of demand and time liabilities so obtained for each half year
shall be applied for arriving at demand and time liabilities components of
savings bank deposits for all reporting fortnights during the next half year.
enriched_requirements.object: at demand and time liabilities components of savings bank deposits for all reporting fortnights during the next half year

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) 'Approved Securities / SLR securities': Following securities shall be considered
as approved securities (approved securities are commonly known as SLR
securities):

(iv) Any other instrument as may be notified by the Reserve Bank of India (as and
when prescribed).
enriched_requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India (as and when prescribed)

enriched_requirements.object: a co-op

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(25) 'Net Liabilities': While computing liabilities for the purpose of CRR and SLR,
the net liabilities of the bank to other banks in India in the 'banking system' shall be
reckoned, i.e., assets in India with other banks in the 'banking system' will be
reduced from total liabilities to the 'banking system.

(26) 'Other Approved Securities' shall mean Government Securities, other than the
securities mentioned in paragraph 6(3) above, subject to the condition that they are
notified as approved securities.
enriched_requirements.object: than the securities mentioned in paragraph 6(3) above
definition

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (31) 'Time Deposits' shall mean deposits other than demand deposits.
enriched_requirements.object: demand deposits

(32) 'Time Liabilities' of a bank shall include those liabilities which are payable
otherwise than on demand and shall include the following:

 All types of time deposits from the banks
 Certificates of deposits from the banks
 Participation Certificates issued to banks which are not payable on
demand
 Interest accrued on time deposits / CDs of banks *
Within the definition of 'banking system'

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(21) 'Liquidity Adjustment Facility (LAF)' shall mean fixed and variable rate Repo
operations (for injection of liquidity) and reverse repo operations (for absorption of
liquidity) conducted by the Reserve Bank of India from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: rate Repo operations (for injection of liquidity) and reverse repo operations (for absorption of liquidity) conducted by the R

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(22) 'Marginal standing facility' shall mean the facility under which the eligible
banks can avail liquidity support from the Reserve Bank against excess SLR
holdings.
enriched_requirements.object: which the eligible banks can avail liquidity support from the Reserve Bank against excess SLR holdings

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(23) 'Market borrowing programme' shall mean the domestic rupee loans raised by
the Government of India and the State Governments from the public and managed
by the Reserve Bank through issue of marketable securities, governed by the
provisions of the Government Securities Act, 2006, Public Debt Act, 1944 and the
Regulations framed under those Acts, through an auction or any other method, as
specified in the notification issued in this regard.
enriched_requirements.object: loans raised by the Government of India and the State Governments from the public and managed by the Reserve Bank through issu

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (24) 'Net balance in current accounts' shall have the same meaning assigned in
enriched_requirements.object: assigned in

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Every bank shall maintain in India by way of cash reserve, a sum equivalent to
such percent of the total of its NDTL in India, in such manner and for such dates, as
the Reserve Bank in terms of Section 42(1) of the RBI Act, 1934 and Section 18(1)
of BR Act, 1949 [including provisions of Section 18 (1) of the BR Act as applicable to
cooperative banks], may specify, by notification in the Official Gazette, from time to
time having regard to the needs of securing the monetary stability in the country.
enriched_requirements.object: way of cash reserve, a sum equivalent to such percent of the total of its NDTL in India, in such manner and for such dates, as th

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: In terms of Section 42(1A) of RBI Act, 1934, the Reserve Bank may require the

scheduled banks to maintain, in addition to the balances prescribed under Section
42(1) of the Act, an additional average daily balance, the amount of which shall not
be less than the rate specified by the Reserve Bank in the notification published in
the Gazette of India from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: rate specified by the Reserve Bank in the notification published in the Gazette of India from time to time

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that such additional balance shall be calculated with reference to the
excess of the total of NDTL of the bank as shown in the Returns referred to in
Section 42(2) of the RBI Act, 1934 over the total of its NDTL at the close of the
business on the date specified in the notification.
enriched_requirements.object: to the excess of the total of NDTL of the bank as shown in the Returns referred to in Section 42(2) of the RBI Act, 1934

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Every scheduled bank shall maintain in India with the Reserve Bank, an average
daily balance, the amount of which shall not be less than 3.75 per cent, 3.5 per cent,

Every co-operative bank, (not being a scheduled co-operative bank), shall
maintain in India on daily basis by way of cash reserve with itself;
enriched_requirements.object: daily basis by way of cash reserve with itself

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
or in one or more of the aforesaid ways, the amount of
<image>
which shall not be less than 3.75 per cent, 3.5 per cent, 3.25 per cent and 3.0 per
cent of its NDTL, as on the last day of the second preceding fortnight, effective from
the reporting fortnight beginning September 6, October 4, November 1 and
November 29, 2025, respectively.

Every scheduled bank shall maintain minimum CRR of not less than 90 percent
of the required CRR on all days during the reporting fortnight, in such a manner that
the average of CRR maintained daily shall not be less than the CRR prescribed by
the Reserve Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: CRR prescribed by the Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of these Directions, the Reserve Bank may specify from time to
time with reference to any transaction or class of transactions that such transaction
or transactions shall be regarded as liability in India of a bank.
enriched_requirements.object: in India of a bank

conditions:
If any question arises as to whether any transaction or transactions shall be
regarded
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If any question arises as to whether any transaction or transactions shall be
regarded, for the purpose of these Directions, as liability in India of a bank, the bank
shall approach the RBI.
enriched_requirements.object: purpose of these Directions, as liability in India of a bank, the bank shall approach the RBI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The decision of the Reserve Bank thereon shall be final.

Loans / borrowings from abroad by banks in India shall be reckoned as
'liabilities to others' and shall be subject to reserve requirements.
enriched_requirements.object: to others' and shall be subject to reserve requirements

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Upper Tier II instruments raised and maintained in India / abroad shall be
reckoned as liability for the computation of NDTL for the purpose of reserve
requirements.
enriched_requirements.object: for the computation of NDTL for the purpose of reserve requirements

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The balance amount in respect of the drafts issued by the accepting bank on its
correspondent bank under the Remittance Facilities Scheme and remaining unpaid
<image>
shall be reckoned as 'Liability to others in India' for the computation of NDTL.
enriched_requirements.object: to others in India' for the computation of NDTL

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The
amount received by correspondent banks shall be reckoned as 'Liability to the
Banking System' and this liability may be netted off by the correspondent banks
against the inter-bank assets.
enriched_requirements.object: to the Banking System' and this liability may be netted off by the correspondent banks against the inter-bank assets

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Sums placed by banks for issuing drafts / interest / dividend warrants shall be
treated as 'Assets with banking system' and banks shall have the option to net them
off from their inter-bank liabilities.
enriched_requirements.object: with banking system' and banks shall have the option to net them off from their inter-bank liabilities

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
scheduled banks in respect of their savings bank deposits on the basis of the
position as at the close of business on 30th September and 31st March every year
shall continue with interest application on savings bank deposits on a daily product
basis.
enriched_requirements.object: on savings bank deposits on a daily product basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The liabilities mentioned below shall not form part of liabilities of a bank for the
purpose of CRR and SLR:
(1) Paid up capital, reserves, borrowings through instruments qualifying for Tier1
and additional Tier1 capital;
enriched_requirements.object: of a bank for the purpose of CRR and SLR: (1) Paid up capital, reserves, borrowings through instruments qualifying for Tier1

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the funds collected by various branches of the bank or other banks for
the issue and held pending finalization of allotment of the additional Tier1 preference
shares shall have to be taken into account for the purpose of calculation of reserve
requirements.
enriched_requirements.object: into account for the purpose of calculation of reserve requirements

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(f) any other financial institution notified by the Central Government in this
behalf
shall be reduced by the aggregate of the liabilities of all such banks and
institutions to the scheduled bank.
enriched_requirements.object: aggregate of the liabilities of all such banks and institutions to the scheduled bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Loans out of Foreign Currency Non-Resident Accounts (Banks), (FCNR [B]
Deposits Scheme) and Inter-Bank Foreign Currency (IBFC) deposits shall be
included as part of bank credit for the purpose of these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: of bank credit for the purpose of these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Banks shall use
the conversion rate announced by Financial Benchmarks India Private Limited (FBIL)
for the purpose of converting foreign assets / liabilities for reporting in the statutory
returns mentioned at paragraphs 4 and 5 of these directions.
enriched_requirements.object: announced by Financial Benchmarks India Private Limited (FBIL) for the purpose of converting foreign assets / liabilities for

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For conversion of
assets / liabilities in other currencies, banks may use New York Closing Rate
pertaining to the day end of the reporting day for converting such currencies into
USD and then use the reference rate of FBIL for USD/ INR for the same day for
conversion into INR.
enriched_requirements.object: Rate pertaining to the day end of the reporting day for converting such currencies into USD and then use the reference rate of FB

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Every bank, in addition to the cash reserves which it is required to maintain
under these Directions, shall maintain in India, assets, the value of which shall
not be less than such percentage not exceeding 40 percent of the total of its
demand and time liabilities in India as on the last day of the second preceding
fortnight as the Reserve Bank may, by notification in the Official Gazette,
specify from time to time and such assets shall be maintained in such form
and such manner, as may be specified in such notification.
enriched_requirements.object: the value of which shall not be less than such percentage not exceeding 40 percent of the total of its demand and time liab

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Every Primary co-operative bank shall maintain in India assets (hereinafter
referred to as 'SLR assets') the value of which shall not, at the close of business on
any day, be less than 18 percent of its total net demand and time liabilities in India as
on the last day of the second preceding fortnight in accordance with the method of
valuation specified by RBI from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: of business on any day, be less than 18 percent of its total net demand and time liabilities in India as on the last day of the second

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Banks permitted by Reserve Bank shall have the option to participate in the
Marginal Standing Facility (MSF) Scheme introduced by the Reserve Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: participate in the Marginal Standing Facility (MSF) Scheme introduced by the Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The
features of the scheme are:
(1) The eligible banks shall have the option to borrow up to two per cent of their
respective NDTL outstanding at the end of the second preceding fortnight.
enriched_requirements.object: borrow up to two per cent of their respective NDTL outstanding at the end of the second preceding fortnight

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The eligible entities shall also continue to access overnight funds under this
facility against their excess SLR holdings.
enriched_requirements.object: overnight funds under this facility against their excess SLR holdings

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) In the event of banks' SLR holding falling below the statutory requirement up to
two percent of their NDTL, banks shall not have the obligation to seek a specific
waiver for default in SLR compliance arising out of use of this facility in terms of
notification issued under sub section (2A) of Section 24 of the Banking Regulation
Act, 1949.
enriched_requirements.object: seek a specific waiver for default in SLR compliance arising out of use of this facility in terms of notification issued under
(Urban) Co-operative banks as under: (1) Cash, or

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Unencumbered investment in approved securities as defined in section 5(a) of
the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (10 of 1949) read with section 56 thereof:
Provided that the instruments that have been acquired from Reserve Bank of India
under reverse repo, shall be considered as eligible assets for SLR maintenance.
enriched_requirements.object: assets for SLR maintenance

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided further that the following securities shall not be treated as encumbered
for the purpose of maintenance of SLR assets, namely
enriched_requirements.object: for the purpose of maintenance of SLR assets, namely

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Funds borrowed under repo including tri-party repo in government securities
shall be exempted from CRR/SLR computation and the security acquired
under repo shall be eligible for SLR provided the security is primarily eligible
for SLR as per the provisions of the Act under which it is required to be
maintained.
enriched_requirements.object: computation and the security acquired under repo shall be eligible for SLR provided the security is primarily eligible for SL

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Borrowings by a bank through repo in corporate bonds and debentures shall
be reckoned as liabilities for Cash Reserve Ratio/ Statutory Liquidity Ratio
requirement and, to the extent these liabilities are to the banking system, they
<image>
shall be netted as per Section 42 (1) (d) of the RBI Act, 1934.
enriched_requirements.object: for Cash Reserve Ratio/ Statutory Liquidity Ratio requirement and, to the extent these liabilities are to the banking syste

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) All banks shall maintain investments in Government Securities only in
Subsidiary General Ledger (SGL) Accounts with Reserve Bank or in CSGL
Accounts of scheduled commercial banks, Primary Dealers (PDs), State Co-
operative Banks, and Stock Holding Corporation of India Ltd.(SHCIL) or in the
dematerialised accounts with depositories such as National Securities
Depositories Ltd (NSDL), Central Depository Services Ltd.
enriched_requirements.object: Securities only in Subsidiary General Ledger (SGL) Accounts with Reserve Bank or in CSGL Accounts of scheduled comm

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(v) 6Banks shall report the SDF balances held by banks with RBI in Form I, as it
is an eligible asset for SLR maintenance.
enriched_requirements.object: held by banks with RBI in Form I, as it is an eligible asset for SLR maintenance

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The balances held by banks with
RBI under the SDF shall not be eligible for Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR)
maintenance.
enriched_requirements.object: Reserve Ratio (CRR) maintenance

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) The cash management bill shall be treated as Government of India Treasury
Bill and thus be reckoned as SLR security.
enriched_requirements.object: of India Treasury Bill and thus be reckoned as SLR security

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The procedure for computation of NDTL for SLR shall be as below:
(1) Total NDTL for the purpose of SLR under Section 24 (2A) of Banking
Regulation Act, 1949, shall be computed on the similar procedure as followed for
CRR.
enriched_requirements.object: Total NDTL for the purpose of SLR under Section 24 (2A) of Banking Regulation Act, 1949, shall be computed on the similar procedure a

all maturities in 'Assets with the Banking System' for computation of NDTL for SLR
purpose.
enriched_requirements.object: of term deposits and term lending of all maturities in 'Assets with the Banking System' for computation of NDTL for SL

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Classification and valuation of approved securities shall be in accordance with
the extant instructions Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks -
Classification, Valuation and Operation of Investment Portfolio) Directions, 2025, as
applicable.
enriched_requirements.object: the extant instructions Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Classification, Valuation and Operation of Investment

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Under Section 42(2) of the RBI Act, 1934, every Scheduled Primary Co-
operative bank shall submit the above-mentioned Return in Form 'B' (Annex I) at the
close of business on last day of each fortnight within five days after the date to which
it relates.

Where such last day of each fortnight is a public holiday under the Negotiable
Instruments Act, 1881, for one or more offices of the bank, the Return shall give at
the close of business of the preceding working day's figure in respect of such office
or offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate to last day of such fortnight.
enriched_requirements.object: of business of the preceding working day's figure in respect of such office or offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate t

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Every co-operative bank, not being a scheduled co-operative bank, shall submit
a Return in Form I (Annex II) together with Appendix I, to the regional office
concerned of the Reserve Bank, not later than 20 days after the end of the month to
which it relates showing the position, inter alia, of cash reserves maintained by the
bank under Section 18 of the B.R.

a public holiday under Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881 for one or more offices of
the bank, the Return shall give the preceding day's figure in respect of such office or
offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate to last day of such fortnight.

if any, for each day of the month

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Whenever there are wide variations between the sources and uses of funds as
being reported in the fortnightly Return and the variations exceed 20 per cent, the
banks concerned should give reasons therefor in the Return.
enriched_requirements.object: the Return

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In terms of Regulation 5(i) (c) of the Scheduled Banks Regulations,1951 and
Regulation 4(1) of the Banking Regulation (Co-operative Societies) Rules, 1966, the
banks are required to furnish a list of the names, the officials designations and
specimen signatures of the officers of the banks who are authorized to sign on behalf
of the banks, Returns prescribed under Section 42(2) of the RBI Act, 1934, and
Section 18 and 24 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
enriched_requirements.object: of the names, the officials designations and specimen signatures of the officers of the banks who are authorized to sign on behalf o

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
11Under the new reporting structure, as amended vide Banking Laws
(Amendment) Act 2025, there shall be no Provisional or Final or Special Form B
return.
enriched_requirements.object: Final or Special Form B return

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The banks are required to submit single Form B return.
enriched_requirements.object: B return

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The banks are required to submit
fortnightly Form B return from December 15, 2025 and monthly Form I return from
December 2025 on the CIMS portal.
enriched_requirements.object: B return from December 15, 2025 and monthly Form I return from December 2025 on the CIMS portal

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
From the subsequent
fortnights starting from January 16, 2026, the maintenance shall be done, as
presently applicable, i.e., based on the NDTL as on the last day of the second
preceding fortnight
38 C.
enriched_requirements.object: applicable, i

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the banks
11 Inserted with effect from December 15, 2025 vide Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) (Amendment) Directions, 2025 dated December 11, 2025 12 Inserted with effect from December 15, 2025 vide Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) (Amendment) Directions, 2025 dated December 11, 2025 13 Amended with effect from December 12, 2025 vide Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) (Amendment) Directions, 2025 dated December 11, 2025

shall maintain minimum CRR of not less than hundred per cent of the required CRR
during the transition period.The banks shall also submit Form B return for December
12, 2025, as per the extant reporting structure.
enriched_requirements.object: not less than hundred per cent of the required CRR during the transition period

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All Co-operative Banks (scheduled and non-scheduled), are required to submit
a Return in Form I (Annex II) under Section 24 of the BR Act, 1949 (AACS) every
month showing the position of liquid assets maintained under the said Section as at
the close of business on last day of each fortnight during the month not later than
twenty days after the end of the month to which it relates.
enriched_requirements.object: in Form I (Annex II) under Section 24 of the BR Act, 1949 (AACS) every month showing the position of liquid assets maintained under

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All Primary Co-operative Banks (scheduled and non-scheduled) are required to
furnish Appendix II, as per proforma given, together with the Return in Form I
showing the position of -
(1) statutory liquid assets required to be maintained under Section 24 of the BR
Act,1949 (AACS).
enriched_requirements.object: as per proforma given, together with the Return in Form I showing the position of - (1) statutory liquid assets required to be

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All Primary Co-operative Banks (scheduled and non-scheduled) should furnish
the information w.r.t.

The monthly Return should contain information of the
fortnights following in the respective months.
enriched_requirements.object: fortnights following in the respective months

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Statutory Auditors shall verify and certify that all items of outside liabilities,
as per the bank's books had been duly compiled by the bank and correctly reflected
under NDTL in the fortnightly/monthly statutory returns submitted to Reserve Bank
for the financial year.
enriched_requirements.object: all items of outside liabilities, as per the bank's books had been duly compiled by the bank and correctly reflected under NDT

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All co-operative banks shall maintain a register, as per format given in Annex
III showing the daily position of cash reserve and liquid assets maintained under
Sections 18 and 24 of the Banking regulation Act, 1949 read with Section 56 thereof
which shall be put up daily to Chief Executive Officer, who is responsible for ensuring
compliance with the statutory liquidity requirements at the close of business every
day.
enriched_requirements.object: per format given in Annex III showing the daily position of cash reserve and liquid assets maintained under Sections 18 and 24

conditions: if the shortfall continues on the next succeeding day/s
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(1) Penal interest shall be recovered from all Scheduled Primary (Urban) Co-
operative Banks in the event of shortfall in maintenance of prescribed CRR on a
daily basis for that day at the rate of three percent per annum above the Bank Rate
on the amount by which the amount actually maintained falls short of the prescribed
minimum on that day and if the shortfall continues on the next succeeding day/s,
penal interest shall be recovered at the rate of five percent per annum above the
Bank Rate.
enriched_requirements.object: Scheduled Primary (Urban) Co- operative Banks in the event of shortfall in maintenance of prescribed CRR on a daily basis for th

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) In the case of a co-operative bank, not being a scheduled co-operative bank, the
bank shall be liable to pay to the Reserve Bank, penal interest as envisaged in sub-
section (1-A) of Section 18 read with Section 56 of the B.R.
enriched_requirements.object: to the Reserve Bank, penal interest as envisaged in sub- section (1-A) of Section 18 read with Section 56 of the B

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Banks are required to furnish the particulars such as date, amount, percentage,
reason for default in maintenance of requisite CRR and also action taken to avoid
recurrence of such default.
enriched_requirements.object: such as date, amount, percentage, reason for default in maintenance of requisite CRR and also action taken to avoid recurre

conditions:
if the
default still continues during the next succeeding fortnight:
(1) Every Director
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Under the provisions of Section 42(3A) of the RBI Act, 1934, penal interest at
the increased rate of five per cent above the Bank Rate become payable and if the
default still continues during the next succeeding fortnight:
(1) Every Director, Manager or Secretary of the scheduled bank who is knowingly
and willfully a party to the default, shall be punishable with fine which may extend to
₹500 and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for each subsequent fortnight
during which default continues.
enriched_requirements.object: which may extend to ₹500 and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for each subsequent fortnight during which default c

conditions:
if default is made by the bank in complying with
the prohibition referred to in this clause
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The Reserve Bank may prohibit a scheduled bank from receiving any fresh
<image>
deposit after the said fortnight, and if default is made by the bank in complying with
the prohibition referred to in this clause, every director and officer of the bank who is
knowingly and willfully a party to such default or who through negligence or otherwise
contributes to such default shall in respect of each such default be punishable with
fine which may extend to ₹500 and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for
each day after the first, on which a deposit received in contravention of such
prohibition is retained by the scheduled bank.
enriched_requirements.object: such default be punishable with fine which may extend to ₹500 and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for each day after t

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Failure to submit the Return/late submission of the Return shall attract the
provisions of Section 42(4) of RBI Act, 1934 and banks are liable for imposition of
penalties as indicated therein.
enriched_requirements.object: Section 42(4) of RBI Act, 1934 and banks are liable for imposition of penalties as indicated therein

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
On the failure of the bank to maintain as on any day, the amount of SLR
required to be maintained by a bank, the bank shall be liable to pay to the Reserve
Bank in respect of that default, the penal interest as envisaged under Section 24
read with Section 56 of the BR Act, 1949.
enriched_requirements.object: to the Reserve Bank in respect of that default, the penal interest as envisaged under Section 24 read with Section 56 of the BR Act,

conditions:
Where it is observed that banks are persistently defaulting despite instructions
and repeated advice

in case of
licensed banks and refuse licence in case of unlicensed banks under Section 22 of
the Act
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where it is observed that banks are persistently defaulting despite instructions
and repeated advice, the Reserve Bank in addition to levy of penalty on such
defaulting banks, may be constrained to consider cancelling the licence in case of
licensed banks and refuse licence in case of unlicensed banks under Section 22 of
the Act, ibid.
enriched_requirements.object: cancelling the licence in case of licensed banks and refuse licence in case of unlicensed banks under Section 22 of the Act

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The banks should, therefore, in their own interest ensure maintenance
of statutory liquidity ratio at prescribed rates and be very prompt in submission of
required Return to Regional Office concerned of Reserve Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: own interest ensure maintenance of statutory liquidity ratio at prescribed rates and be very prompt in submission of required Re

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Disclaimer: It is hereby advised that mere inclusion of any item in the above Master Direction should not be construed as a permission to undertake all such activities by a banking entity.
enriched_requirements.object: permission to undertake all such activities by a banking entity

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Directions,
instructions and guidelines repealed prior to the issuance of these Directions shall
continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue to
be governed by the provisions thereof.
enriched_requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or acknowledgments granted
under these repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: by these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the repeal of these directions, instructions, or guidelines shall not in any way
prejudicially affect:
enriched_requirements.object: affect:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
enriched_requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being
in force
enriched_requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary clarifications in
<image>
respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any provision of these
Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.

conditions: Where last day of the fortnight is a public holiday under the Negotiable Instruments Act
enriched_requirements.full_sentence: @ Where last day of the fortnight is a public holiday under the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881 (26 of 1881) for one or more offices of a scheduled State Co-operative bank, the Return shall give the preceding working day's figures in respect of such office or offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate to last day of that fortnight.
enriched_requirements.object: day's figures in respect of such office or offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate to last day of that fortnigh

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: * The expression "Banking System" or "Banks" wherever it appears in the return means the banks and any other financial institutions referred to in regulations (i) to (v) of clause (e) of the Explanation below Section 42(1) of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.
enriched_requirements.object: other financial institutions referred to in regulations (i) to (v) of clause (e) of the Explanation below Section 42(1) of the Reser

conditions: If it is not possible to provide the figure against I (c) separately from II(c)
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
@@ If it is not possible to provide the figure against I (c) separately from II(c), the same may be
included in the figure against II (c).
enriched_requirements.object: figure against II (c)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:

the Officer(s) submitted the Return :
Statement of Demand and Time liabilities in India and Amount maintained
in India in Cash, Gold and unencumbered Securities for the Month of
The amounts of various items in this return should be worked out after taking into account, where necessary, the adjustments indicated in the footnotes at the end of the return.

conditions: If last day of the fortnight is a holiday under the Negotiable Instruments Act
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If last day of the fortnight is a holiday under the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881 (26
of 1881), the figures as at the close of business on the preceding working day should
be furnished.
For the purposes of this return, "Liabilities in India" shall not include.

For the purpose of this return, the expression "Banking system" shall comprise the
following banks and financial institutions, viz.
enriched_requirements.object: and financial institutions, viz

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of this return, "liabilities in India to others" shall not include borrowings
from a State Government, the Reserve Bank, Export-Import Bank of India, the National
Housing Bank, the Small Industries Bank, National Bank for Agriculture and Rural
Development, or the National Co-operative Development Corporation established
under Section 3 of the National Co- operative Development Act, 1962
% (i) Any balance held by Co-operative Bank with another bank shall not, to the
extent such balance represents the investment of Agricultural Credit Stabilisation Fund
of such Co-operative Bank, be deemed to be cash maintained in India.
enriched_requirements.object: State Government, the Reserve Bank, Export-Import Bank of India, the National Housing Bank, the Small Industries Bank, Natio

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) In case the co-operative bank has taken an advance against any balance
maintained with the State Co-operative bank of the State concerned or with the
District Central Co-operative Bank of the district concerned, such balance to the
extent to which it has been drawn against or availed of shall not be deemed to
be such cash maintained in India.
enriched_requirements.object: such cash maintained in India

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
& (i) For the purpose of this return, any cash with a Co-operative Bank shall not, to
the extent such cash represents the balance in Agricultural Credit Stabilisation
Fund of such Co-operative Bank, be deemed to be cash maintained in India.
enriched_requirements.object: such cash represents the balance in Agricultural Credit Stabilisation Fund of such Co-operative Bank, be deemed to be cash maintained

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Cash must not include balance with other banks or any item other than bank /
currency notes, rupee coin (including one rupee notes) and subsidiary coins
current on the date of this return.
enriched_requirements.object: banks or any item other than bank / currency notes, rupee coin (including one rupee notes) and subsidiary coins current on

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
++ Scheduled State Co-operative Banks should show here only the amount in excess of
the balance required to be maintained with the Reserve Bank of India under Section 42
of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.
enriched_requirements.object: amount in excess of the balance required to be maintained with the Reserve Bank of India under Section 42 of the Reserve Bank of In

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Approved securities, or a portion thereof, representing investment of monies of
Agricultural Credit Stabilisation Fund of a Co-operative Bank, shall not be
deemed to be unencumbered approved securities.
enriched_requirements.object: unencumbered approved securities
